Probably Should've been Disqualified

Helane Becker · Cowen & Co.

JetBlue shares fell just 9% when the merger was blocked — analysts said investors were "breathing a sigh of relief that the purchase of Spirit appears dead."

Wall Street · Fortune

The cost of bad pipeline

40%

of sales time lost to deals
that will never close

Nearly half of all sales activity is spent on opportunities that were never going to convert — driven by optimism, pipeline pressure, or simply not knowing when to walk away.

We are waiting to lose.

Team check-in

Does this reflect your reality?

  • Think about the last 3 deals you lost. How many of them felt like they were never going to close — even when you were still working them?
  • We see 70% of losses happening before SE ever engages. Does that feel right for your segment? Are you getting pulled into deals earlier than you should be?
  • The data says only 11% of losses were Shopify-driven (product gaps). The rest were merchant timing, ghosting, or unknown. Do you feel like product fit is where you lose most, or something else?
  • When you know a deal is wrong — what stops you from DQ'ing it? What does a "swipe left" moment feel like in practice?

DQ early.
Win more.

The best teams don't close everything — they know what not to work. A fast no creates space for a faster yes.

Qualify hard, early Trust the signals Protect your pipeline Move to winnable deals
